Train Your Robot with Reinforcement Learning in ROS 2!
Ever wondered how to get started with Reinforcement Learning (RL) in robotics using ROS 2?
Check out the ros2_flatland_rl_tutorial package created by Filipe Almeida, a hands-on guide to training a robot using Stable-Baselines3, OpenAI Gym, and the Flatland lightweight 2D simulator under ROS 2 Humble.
What you'll learn:
✅ How to set up a custom Gym environment with ROS 2 nodes
✅ How to train a robot to navigate a hallway using the PPO algorithm
✅ How to structure rewards and observations from Lidar for RL
✅ How to simulate faster and evaluate training strategies
🔗 This repository is a great way to dive into Robot Learning, experiment with different environments, and see RL in action!
https://lnkd.in/eF4FAJsK
Do you have other awesome RL + ROS 2 example setup?
Let's connect and share insights 🔽
#ROS2 #AI #Robotics

I’ve built ACPL (Adaptive Consequence Penalized Learning), a reinforcement learning approach designed to explicitly model delayed negative consequences. Instead of optimizing only for immediate reward, it penalizes actions based on anticipated future harm.
The goal is to make RL agents safer and more risk-aware, especially in environments where failures are delayed, noisy, or rare.

comment: I appreciate your thorough breakdown of the arguments in favor of humanoid robots, particularly the points about navigating human-designed environments and the ease of training robots with human-like form.
